windows.embed_text: a tray with three cups and a vase
windows.old_description: Pouring wax into round moulds for dessert (macaron) candles.
windows.embed_text_v2: adding liquid dye and stirring colorant into melted wax in white cups on a bamboo tray, coloring three batches yellow red and burgundy with dropper dye bottle, dessert candle making
windows.summary_v2: A maker adds liquid dye from a dropper bottle into cups of melted wax and stirs to color them while making dessert-style candles.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down on a bamboo tray holding three white cups of melted wax on a whitewashed wood surface: left cup pale yellow, middle bright red, right dark burgundy; a white vase and baby's-breath sit to the right.
A bare hand enters from the left holding a small dropper bottle of dark liquid dye and squeezes a drop into the pale-yellow left cup.
The hand dips the dropper/stir tip into the left cup, beginning to swirl the dark colorant through the yellow wax.
Hand withdraws; the left cup's wax is now tinged darker while the red and burgundy cups sit untouched on the tray.
Hand returns and grips the rim of the left cup, steadying it as the colored wax settles; red and dark-purple cups remain at center and right.
Hand holds the left cup steady on the bamboo tray; the three tinted waxes (now-darkened, red, burgundy) are clearly colored in their white cups.
Hand picks up a small red-capped dye bottle and works near the left cup again, adjusting the color of the melted wax.
Hand grips and slides the left cup forward on the tray; only two cups (red, burgundy) remain fully in frame at right as the colored wax is repositioned.
Hand uncaps/handles the dye bottle by the left cup once more, continuing to add and blend colorant into the melted wax on the tray.
